hero.title: Sketches to Film
hero.description: Transform static storyboards or concept sketches into moving film sequences with AI. Bring hand-drawn frames to life with motion, camera moves, and atmosphere so you can preview how your story will feel on screen.
hero.inputPlaceholder: Upload storyboard or scene sketches to turn into moving footage

steps.title: Upload your storyboard frames or sketches
steps.description: Provide key frames, thumbnails, or simple scene drawings for the moments you care about.

steps.title: Describe motion, timing, and camera work
steps.description: Indicate pan, zoom, dolly, or handheld feels along with desired pacing.

steps.title: Generate animated film sequences from sketches
steps.description: AI interpolates motion and depth from your sketches, producing moving shots that match the drawn composition.

steps.title: Review and refine story pacing
steps.description: Adjust timing, regenerate specific shots, and export sequences for internal reviews and pitches.

items.question: What is the Sketches to Film workflow?
items.answer: Sketches to Film is an AI animatic generator that transforms storyboard sketches into cinematic video sequences. It bridges the gap between static boards and moving images.

items.question: What inputs do I need for this template?
items.answer: You upload your storyboard or concept sketches, then optionally add text descriptions about mood, camera movement, and pacing. The workflow uses both the drawings and your notes to generate video.

items.question: Can I use rough hand-drawn sketches?
items.answer: Yes. Rough sketches are fine as long as the composition and main subjects are clear. The AI focuses on the structure and layout rather than perfect drawing quality.

items.question: What type of output does Sketches to Film create?
items.answer: The workflow generates short video clips or sequences that approximate the motion, framing, and atmosphere of your storyboard. They’re ideal for previs and concept validation.

items.question: Is this suitable for previsualization (previs) workflows?
items.answer: Absolutely. It’s designed as a previs workflow so directors, storyboard artists, and producers can see a quick moving version of their boards without a full animation team.

items.question: How much control do I have over style and genre?
items.answer: You can specify style and genre through prompts (e.g., “gritty sci-fi,” “soft fantasy,” “anime-inspired”). These style hints influence how the AI interprets your sketches in the final video.

items.question: Can I iterate on a single scene multiple times?
items.answer: Yes. You can duplicate runs, tweak prompts or sketches, and generate alternate versions of the same scene to compare options before committing.
